jokes.text: Will, classes have been in session for five weeks...and you're buying books now? / Yeah, I thought I'd get an early start this year.
jokes.setup: Carlton points out Will is buying books 5 weeks into the semester
jokes.punchline: Will calls this an 'early start'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Will's street-smart confidence applied to academic procrastination. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: Human Sexuality 420. / Four-twenty? That's a graduate course. / Oh, yeah, I did advance placement in high school. You know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Will lists a graduate-level sexuality course, Carlton notes it's advanced
jokes.punchline: Will's suggestive 'advance placement' claim with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Classic Will sexual bravado joke with good setup/payoff structure. The 'you know what I mean' sells it.

jokes.text: See, we got this little game that we play. You know, I ask her out and she says no, you know. Right now, she's winning 64-zip, but I feel lucky.
jokes.setup: Will describes his relationship with Valerie as a 'game'
jokes.punchline: The absurd score of 64-0 rejections but still feeling optimistic
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing - perfectly captures Will's delusional confidence. Sports metaphor is clever and specific score makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, why? Let me guess, you're not gonna ask me out today.
jokes.setup: Will says it's her lucky day
jokes.punchline: Valerie's sarcastic hope that he won't ask her out for once
jokes.explanation: Good comeback line that establishes their dynamic. Economical and character-driven.

jokes.text: Oh, okay. Hey, you're fired.
jokes.setup: Valerie says she can't go out because she has plans with her cousin
jokes.punchline: Will's non-sequitur response treating her like an employee
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Will response. Maximum economy - two words that completely subvert expectations.

jokes.text: What's the difference? If you don't go out with me, you're gonna go out with me, you're gonna go out with her or her or her.
jokes.setup: Valerie questions the difference between single and double dates
jokes.punchline: Will's logic that she'll end up with him eventually through various scenarios
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's persistent confidence but the execution is a bit muddled. Character-appropriate but not sharp.

jokes.text: Now, come on, I mean, a guy can lie with his lips...but he can't lie with his eyes. [Will makes exaggerated puppy dog eyes]
jokes.setup: Will delivers a romantic line about honesty in his eyes
jokes.punchline: The visual of Will making ridiculous over-the-top pleading eyes
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith physical comedy moment. The contrast between smooth words and silly expression works.

jokes.text: Will, I will go out with you. But it's not because of those fool eyes you just gave me. Because I got no place else to go.
jokes.setup: Will thinks his puppy dog eyes worked
jokes.punchline: Valerie's brutal honesty that she's only going because she's desperate
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Will's ego. Good structure that builds him up then cuts him down.

jokes.text: Why don't y'all roll past the crib...you know, around 8:00? Give me time to pad the headboard.
jokes.setup: Will is setting up logistics for the date
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about 'padding the headboard'
jokes.explanation: Typical Will sexual bravado. Character-appropriate but somewhat predictable innuendo.

jokes.text: I'm kidding. You're sensitive, baby.
jokes.setup: Will makes sexual joke, presumably gets negative reaction
jokes.punchline: Will backtracks and calls her sensitive
jokes.explanation: Minor callback moment that shows Will's pattern of backing down when called out.

jokes.text: Oh, please. I seen you out with plenty of women...who looked like they had a little chihuahua in them.
jokes.setup: Carlton complains about blind dates usually being 'dogs'
jokes.punchline: Will's specific insult about Carlton's dates looking like chihuahuas
jokes.explanation: Good roast that builds on Carlton's 'dogs' comment. The specific breed makes it funnier than generic insult.

jokes.text: If you're referring to Conchita, I don't see her anymore.
jokes.setup: Will insults Carlton's dating choices
jokes.punchline: Carlton's serious acknowledgment that confirms Will's point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - earnest and inadvertently confirming the insult. Good economy.

jokes.text: Carlton's defensive response about Conchita
jokes.setup: Will's insult about Carlton dating unattractive women
jokes.punchline: Carlton's specific denial about one particular woman
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Carlton's defensive nature and giving a specific name makes it funnier than a generic response.

jokes.text: You know, this housework stuff's kind of fun. Heh. / There's another one.
jokes.setup: Hilary thinks she's helping with housework
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey points out another spot she missed, showing she's useless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character moment - obliviously incompetent at basic tasks. Geoffrey's dry delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Since I married Jewel, I'm a changed man. Unless the double date's with Hilary.
jokes.setup: Jazz claims he's a changed man since marriage
jokes.punchline: He'd still make an exception for Hilary despite being married
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Jazz's established character trait. Economical setup and punchline structure.

jokes.text: Hey, brown sugar. [HELICOPTER WHIRRING] I gotta go.
jokes.setup: Jazz flirts with Hilary
jokes.punchline: Helicopter arrives and he has to leave mid-flirtation for police chase
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment. The helicopter interrupting romance is unexpected and character-appropriate for Jazz.

jokes.text: So you couldn't find anybody else? I guess I'm your only friend. How pathetic.
jokes.setup: Will asks Carlton to go on the double date
jokes.punchline: Carlton insults Will while simultaneously agreeing to help him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - superior attitude while still being helpful. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: When he's in the passenger seat, he turns ugly. 'You're going fast. You're going slow.' [IN DEEP VOICE] 'Take a left. Take a right.' [IN DEEP VOICE] 'I don't care how pretty she is, you can't drive on the sidewalk.'
jokes.setup: Will describes Uncle Phil as a bad passenger
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of complaints ending with absurd sidewalk driving scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure with perfect final punchline. The voice impression adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Look, Ashley, get out while you can. / Whoop, too late.
jokes.setup: Will warns Ashley about driving with Phil
jokes.punchline: Phil appears just as Will says it's too late
jokes.explanation: Good timing-based joke. Economy of language makes the punchline land well.

jokes.text: What's wrong with her? / I don't know. It had nothing to do with me. I wasn't around. / I think she's drinking.
jokes.setup: Phil asks what's wrong with Ashley after she runs away
jokes.punchline: Carlton's over-defensive denial followed by Will's absurd explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor. Carlton's guilt and Will's absurd diagnosis work well together.

jokes.text: Would you like to ride In my beautiful balloon? We could float among the stars togeth... [STOPS MUSIC] What the hell are you doing?
jokes.setup: Carlton sets romantic mood with cheesy music
jokes.punchline: Will's harsh interruption cutting off the romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Carlton's earnest romanticism and Will's bluntness. Good cringe moment.

jokes.text: Carlton setting romantic mood with balloon ride music, Will shutting it down: 'this is my date'
jokes.setup: Carlton plays romantic music to set the mood
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt correction about whose date it actually is
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Carlton's romantic enthusiasm and Will's territorial reality check. Solid character-based humor.

jokes.text: No, I don't think you understand. See, this is my date. You're just here running interference while I get my mack on.
jokes.setup: Carlton thinks it's a joint romantic evening
jokes.punchline: Will explains Carlton is just a distraction while he pursues Valerie
jokes.explanation: Classic Will selfishness. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Excuse me, Superboy? Scratch the cool. You just chill a little bit. All right?
jokes.setup: Will tells Carlton to look cool
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives up on that plan and just tells him to 'chill'
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's lack of confidence in Carlton's abilities. The 'Superboy' nickname is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Mm. God bless America.
jokes.setup: The girls arrive and Will sees them
jokes.punchline: Will's patriotic appreciation of attractive women
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction. Good economy but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Which one of these losers is my date? / That would be me. / Where's the rest of him?
jokes.setup: Karen rudely asks which loser is her date
jokes.punchline: Her immediate height insult when Carlton identifies himself
jokes.explanation: Brutal but effective height joke. Karen's bluntness establishes her character immediately.

jokes.text: Karen's height insult: 'Where's the rest of him?' about Carlton
jokes.setup: Carlton is introduced as Karen's date
jokes.punchline: Karen's immediate height-based insult
jokes.explanation: Standard short joke but delivered with Karen's characteristic meanness. Not particularly original but serves the character dynamic.

jokes.text: I may be short, but you know what they say...about short men and tall women, don't you? / No, what? / I don't know, just making conversation.
jokes.setup: Carlton starts confident comeback about short men and tall women
jokes.punchline: He admits he doesn't actually know any saying, was just making conversation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectation. Sets up like a comeback then completely deflates. Perfect Carlton character moment - trying to be suave but failing awkwardly.

jokes.text: I got this crick in my neck right here that's been really killing me. / Let me help you out with that, cuz. [CARLTON GRUNTS] We do this all the time. / Carlton, get off me. Get off. Get off me!
jokes.setup: Will mentions neck problem, Carlton offers to help
jokes.punchline: Carlton awkwardly massaging Will in front of the dates
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that creates awkwardness. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: No, this is 9½ Weeks. / That is a sex movie. / Really? I thought it was a documentary about Vanilla Ice's career.
jokes.setup: Karen identifies 9½ Weeks as a sex movie
jokes.punchline: Carlton's innocent misunderstanding about Vanilla Ice documentary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection joke. Perfect Carlton naivety combined with topical pop culture reference. The logic actually makes sense which makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Are you crying? / Um... No, I think I've got something in my eye.
jokes.setup: Carlton is emotional watching Old Yeller
jokes.punchline: Carlton's embarrassed denial with classic excuse
jokes.explanation: Very predictable joke but works for Carlton's sensitive character. Clichéd but appropriate.

jokes.text: Well, you know, sometimes I be at the crib alone...and I just be crying for nothing.
jokes.setup: Valerie encourages showing feelings
jokes.punchline: Will's oversharing about random crying sessions
jokes.explanation: Good oversharing moment that shows Will's attempt to be vulnerable backfiring.

jokes.text: What a punk.
jokes.setup: Will admits to crying randomly
jokes.punchline: Karen's blunt, dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Karen response. Maximum economy and establishes her harsh personality clearly.

jokes.text: You know, in case you haven't heard...tar and nicotine are damaging to your lungs. It isn't just a rumor, it will give you a tumor.
jokes.setup: Karen lights up a cigarette
jokes.punchline: Carlton's earnest anti-smoking PSA with forced rhyme
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - earnest do-gooder with awkward delivery. The rhyme is corny but intentionally so.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go outside and fire up a butt. / Wow, that sounds like fun. Huh? Huh?
jokes.setup: Karen says she's going to smoke outside
jokes.punchline: Will's desperate attempt to sound interested in anything she does
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's desperation to connect with the difficult date. Character-driven but not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: I think he wants you to leave. / I don't want her to leave. I want you to leave, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Karen interprets Will's neck massage request as wanting her to leave
jokes.punchline: Will clarifies he wants Carlton to leave, not her
jokes.explanation: Good clarification joke that shows Will's priorities. Functional relationship humor.

jokes.text: Fine. Oh. And they shoot the damn dog.
jokes.setup: Carlton is upset about being asked to leave during Old Yeller
jokes.punchline: Carlton spoils the movie ending out of spite
jokes.explanation: Brilliant petty revenge moment. Perfect Carlton passive-aggressive response. The casual cruelty is unexpected and hilarious.

jokes.text: I cannot believe you and Will are related. You're so different. / Like you and your cousin.
jokes.setup: Valerie comments on how different Carlton and Will are
jokes.punchline: Carlton points out she and Karen are equally different
jokes.explanation: Good observational comeback that creates symmetry. Shows Carlton's social awareness.

jokes.text: Yeah, thanks for putting up with her. / You know, most men find Karen kind of... / Scary. / Sort of.
jokes.setup: Carlton searches for diplomatic way to describe Karen
jokes.punchline: Valerie's blunt 'scary' assessment, Carlton's mild agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's politeness versus Valerie's directness.

jokes.text: No, this ain't Carlton, this is Will. / Oh, Valerie. Yeah, yeah, you know what? I got some choice words for you too, girl. How you gonna j...? Hello?
jokes.setup: Will answers Carlton's phone, expecting to confront Valerie
jokes.punchline: She hangs up before he can finish his angry response
jokes.explanation: Good deflation moment. Will's anger is cut short by the hang-up.

jokes.text: Master William...if you're serious, I could make a call. / I was just kidding, G. / Me too.
jokes.setup: Will says he could kill Carlton
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey offers to arrange it, then claims he was kidding too
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor from Geoffrey. The double 'me too' suggests he might not be kidding. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Maybe you should talk to somebody. / You're right. Look, Nicky, it's like this, man. We always make... / Not me. I'm just a kid.
jokes.setup: Nicky suggests Will should talk to somebody about his problems
jokes.punchline: When Will starts to confide in him, Nicky clarifies he meant someone else
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Nicky gives advice then immediately disclaims responsibility. Perfect kid logic.

jokes.text: Driving lessons. / Lesson number one: Use a car.
jokes.setup: Phil says he's giving Ashley driving lessons
jokes.punchline: Will points out they're not using a car (presumably sitting on couch)
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag setup. Perfect economy - three words that deflate the whole scenario.

jokes.text: Ashley. / What? / You're in neutral.
jokes.setup: Ashley is pretending to drive
jokes.punchline: Phil has to point out basic driving mistake in imaginary scenario
jokes.explanation: Continues the visual gag premise. Phil taking it seriously makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Ooh, cute boy, 3 o'clock. Ha, ha. / Keep your eyes on the road.
jokes.setup: They're pretending to drive down Sunset Boulevard
jokes.punchline: Hilary gets distracted by imaginary cute boy, Ashley reminds her to focus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character moment - getting distracted by men even in imaginary scenarios.

jokes.text: Oh, look, there's Bruce and Demi. Hi. / Yuck, what is she wearing?
jokes.setup: Hilary waves at imaginary celebrities
jokes.punchline: Immediately criticizes Demi Moore's imaginary outfit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary shallow celebrity obsession. The quick turn from friendly to catty is very in character.

jokes.text: Stop. / What? / Cartier's having a sale. Pick me up in an hour.
jokes.setup: They're in the middle of imaginary driving lesson
jokes.punchline: Hilary 'stops' for imaginary shopping, abandoning Ashley
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the imaginary driving bit. Hilary abandoning Ashley for shopping is peak character behavior.

jokes.text: Turn your back, Carlton's liable to start eating and say: 'Oops. Dad, it just jumped in my mouth.'
jokes.setup: Phil warns Carlton to be careful with the drumstick
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Carlton would steal food and make absurd excuse
jokes.explanation: Good character attack from Will. The excuse is absurd enough to be funny.

jokes.text: That wouldn't happen if you treated the drumstick like a person...instead of a piece of meat.
jokes.setup: Will accuses Carlton of being likely to steal food
jokes.punchline: Carlton's earnest response treating the drumstick with moral consideration
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Carlton response. Taking moral high ground about a piece of chicken is peak Carlton.

jokes.text: You know what, Carlton? You ain't nothing but a dog. / Oh, yeah? Well, you're an acrimonious, rancorous, virulent coveter. / Oh, yeah? Well, you ain't nothing but a dog.
jokes.setup: Will calls Carlton a dog with simple insult
jokes.punchline: Carlton responds with elaborate vocabulary, Will repeats same simple insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast. Carlton's verbose intellectualism versus Will's street simplicity. The repetition makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I like Dad's stories. / And some day, I'm gonna tell my kids these same pointless anecdotes.
jokes.setup: Nicky expresses enthusiasm for Phil's stories
jokes.punchline: Carlton's backhanded compliment calling them 'pointless anecdotes'
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton sarcasm that shows his intelligence while still being affectionate toward Phil.

jokes.text: Hold up a second there, Aesop. What's this apartment stuff? Thought you grew up on a farm.
jokes.setup: Phil tells story about growing up in apartment
jokes.punchline: Will catches inconsistency with Phil's usual farm stories
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Phil's inconsistent stories. 'Aesop' nickname is clever reference to his moralizing.

jokes.text: Well, in the South, 'farm' and 'apartment' are interchangeable.
jokes.setup: Will points out Phil's story inconsistency
jokes.punchline: Phil's ridiculous explanation that the terms are interchangeable in the South
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist defense from Phil. The confidence in delivering obvious nonsense makes it work.

jokes.text: Okay, okay, I made it up. All right? Pfft. / Huh. I guess lying just run all through this family, huh?
jokes.setup: Phil admits he made up the story
jokes.punchline: Will suggests lying is a family trait, implying Carlton lies too
jokes.explanation: Good transition back to the main conflict. Will uses Phil's admission to attack Carlton.

jokes.text: I'm not lying, you nefarious, degenerate miscreant. / Oh, like I'm supposed to believe that, you big dog.
jokes.setup: Carlton denies lying with elaborate vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Will dismisses it and returns to calling Carlton a dog
jokes.explanation: Continues the established pattern of Carlton's verbose responses versus Will's simple insults.

jokes.text: Did you put super glue in my hair gel?
jokes.setup: Carlton discovering his hands are stuck to his head
jokes.punchline: Revelation of Will's specific prank method
jokes.explanation: Escalates the conflict with a specific prank. Visual comedy of Carlton stuck to his own head.

jokes.text: Hey, that's what you get for stealing my girl.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking about the hair gel prank
jokes.punchline: Will's unapologetic justification
jokes.explanation: Straightforward justification that shows Will's lack of remorse and keeps the conflict central.

jokes.text: Will's comeback: 'So I'm the embarrassment now?' followed by Carlton's height jabs
jokes.setup: Carlton criticizes Will's dating style as embarrassing
jokes.punchline: Their argument escalating to personal attacks about height and maturity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their conflict with character-appropriate insults. The height jokes are predictable but serve the building tension.

jokes.text: I didn't have to steal her. She was bored with you so she came to me, a real man. / So, what you saying? I'm not a real man? / Yeah, you got it.
jokes.setup: Carlton claims Valerie chose him as 'a real man'
jokes.punchline: Carlton bluntly confirms Will isn't a real man when asked directly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment. Carlton's unusual assertiveness creates conflict.

jokes.text: You should know...that all that 'yo, yo, hey, baby, bap, bap, bap' stuff is just played out. It worked in high school, but now it's just embarrassing.
jokes.setup: Carlton continues attacking Will's manhood
jokes.punchline: Carlton's accurate criticism of Will's outdated pickup style with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Good character insight. Carlton accurately dissecting Will's persona is unusual and effective.

jokes.text: Take it easy, stumpy. / You know something else? I'm tired of all those short jokes. I'm average height. / For a woman.
jokes.setup: Will calls Carlton 'stumpy,' Carlton defends his height as average
jokes.punchline: Will's brutal clarification 'for a woman'
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and brutal punchline. Will lets Carlton set himself up then delivers perfect comeback.

jokes.text: I'm large enough for Valerie.
jokes.setup: Will continues height attacks
jokes.punchline: Carlton's confident assertion about being adequate for Valerie
jokes.explanation: Good comeback attempt from Carlton. Shows his newfound confidence with sexual undertone.

jokes.text: Pfft. Like that hurt.
jokes.setup: Will presumably gets hurt in the fight with Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will's macho denial while clearly in pain
jokes.explanation: Standard macho denial joke. Predictable but appropriate for the situation.

jokes.text: Damn, still alive. / You know what keeps me going? The thought of dancing on your grave.
jokes.setup: Will expresses disappointment Carlton survived
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dark response about dancing on Will's grave
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor escalation. Shows how bitter their fight has become.

jokes.text: Oh, hey, good thing too, because I'm feeling dirty. Ha-ha-ha. / Tootsie roll.
jokes.setup: Nurse mentions sponge bath
jokes.punchline: Will's sexual innuendo about feeling dirty, nurse's dismissive 'tootsie roll' response
jokes.explanation: Typical Will sexual humor. The nurse's dismissive response deflates his ego effectively.

jokes.text: First you steal my girl and now you're gonna steal my nurse. / You old prune. I ought to knock your teeth out. / Go ahead, they're in the glass next to my bed.
jokes.setup: Will threatens to knock Carlton's teeth out
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals his teeth are already out, in a glass
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion. Carlton deflates Will's threat with unexpected reveal. Great setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: I'm feeling the life being sucked out of me. Before I die I just want you to know that I... I hate you. Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Will appears to be making deathbed confession to Carlton
jokes.punchline: Instead of love confession, he says he hates Carlton then laughs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. Sets up like a touching moment then completely subverts it. Perfect Will pettiness.

jokes.text: I have a nephew with a possibly broken nose. And I don't wanna even know why your damn hands are stuck to your head.
jokes.setup: Phil surveys the damage from Will and Carlton's fight
jokes.punchline: Phil's exasperated observation about Carlton's glued hands situation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier prank. Phil's resignation is relatable parental humor.

jokes.text: I should know you would never do to me the kind of things that I would do to you.
jokes.setup: Will is apologizing to Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will's admission that he judges Carlton by his own low standards
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware moment. Will acknowledging his own moral failings is both funny and touching.

jokes.text: It was a lucky shot. / I hit you pretty square, Will. / Look, the important thing is that we get beyond that lucky punch.
jokes.setup: Will calls Carlton's punch lucky
jokes.punchline: Carlton corrects him that it was accurate, Will insists on calling it lucky again
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency. Will can't admit Carlton beat him fairly, even while apologizing.

jokes.text: You're right, man. I'm sorry about the hair. Let me help you. [RIPPING] [CARLTON SCREAMING]
jokes.setup: Will offers to help remove the glue from Carlton's hair
jokes.punchline: Will yanks Carlton's hair out, causing more pain
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy ending. Will's attempt to help making things worse is appropriately painful.
